name: Apache - Alert on Critical Server Errors
xql_query:
datamodel dataset = apache_httpd_raw
// 1. Explicitly select all relevant XDM fields for SOAR mapping (from Error Log section)
| fields _time, _product, _vendor,
xdm.source.ipv4, xdm.source.ipv6, xdm.source.process.pid, xdm.source.process.thread_id,
xdm.source.host.hostname, xdm.observer.name, xdm.target.ipv4,
xdm.event.log_level, xdm.event.description, xdm.target.resource.id
// 2. Filter for CRITICAL or ALERT log levels
| filter xdm.event.log_level in ("CRITICAL", "ALERT")
// 3. Ensure critical grouping fields are not null
| filter xdm.event.description != null and xdm.observer.name != null
// 4. Aggregate the counts by Error Description and Observer Name (Server Hostname)
| comp count(_time) as error_event_count,
values(xdm.source.process.pid) as affected_pids,
values(xdm.source.process.thread_id) as affected_tids,
min(_time) as first_occurrence,
max(_time) as last_occurrence,
// --- Contextual XDM Fields carried forward using min() ---
min(xdm.target.ipv4) as server_ip,
min(xdm.source.ipv4) as client_ip,
min(xdm.event.log_level) as log_level
by xdm.event.description, xdm.observer.name
// 5. Set the threshold for alerting (e.g., 3 occurrences of the same critical error)
| filter error_event_count >= 3
// 6. Select the aggregated results and grouping fields, retaining full context
| fields *
description: This query identifies high-severity error log events (crit or alert) that could indicate a serious server or application misconfiguration requiring immediate attention.
alert_name: Apache - Alert on Critical Server Errors
